title: Joby Aviation Clears FAA Stage 4 Certification
text: Joby Aviation cleared Stage 4 of its FAA type certification process in late March 2026, marking significant progress toward commercial operations. The company's test aircraft completed more than 850 flights in 2025—a 260 percent increase over 2024. The flight data and insights accumulated in 2025 are directly supporting the final stages of Joby's FAA certification efforts, including the compliance data needed to begin Type Inspection Authorization testing in 2026. On November 5, Joby began power-on testing of the first of several FAA-conforming aircraft to be built for type inspection authorization. The company expects FAA-witnessed compliance flight testing to be followed by Type Certificate issuance, estimated for late 2026, which constitutes final FAA confirmation that the S4 design meets all applicable airworthiness standards. Vertiports at Dubai International Airport, set to be completed in Q1 2026, will make up the initial vertiport network for Joby's air taxi service in Dubai, set to launch in 2026. Joby's goal is carrying its first passengers in 2026 as the company progresses through final certification stages.
